project_type: Python AWS CDK v2 project - event-driven sleep audio processing pipeline (serverless)
build_system: pip for dependencies, CDK CLI (via npx) for synthesis. NODE_OPTIONS='' must prefix CDK/Node commands in sandbox.
test_framework: pytest with aws_cdk.assertions for CDK template tests, unittest.mock for Lambda handler tests. Shared fixtures in tests/conftest.py.
verification_instructions: 1. Run pytest tests/ --cov=cdk_base --cov=lambda -q to verify all tests pass with coverage. 2. Run NODE_OPTIONS='' npx cdk synth -c environment=dev --quiet to verify CDK synthesis. 3. Confirm no test regressions (298+ tests passing).
setup_instructions: pip install -r requirements.txt -r requirements-dev.txt boto3 botocore
environment_constraints: COMMON_DEPENDENCIES network mode. Python 3.9 is default in sandbox (docs say 3.11 but tests run fine on 3.9). NODE_OPTIONS='' must be set before any Node.js/CDK commands.
contribution_requirements: TDD-first: write failing tests before implementation. Single-concern PRs. Conventional commits (feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:, refactor:). All changes must align with ARCHITECTURE.md. Multi-environment validation required.
key_patterns: CDK stack in cdk_base/cdk_base_stack.py. Lambda handler in lambda/sleep_audio_processor/handler.py. Tests use conftest.py fixtures for template generation per environment. Lambda tests mock boto3 clients via patch.object(handler, 'client_name', mock). State machine definition tests parse Fn::Join arrays from CloudFormation.
directory_structure: app.py (CDK entry), cdk_base/ (stack code), lambda/sleep_audio_processor/ (Lambda handler), tests/unit/ (14 test files), .github/workflows/ci.yml (CI)
